Deforestation Trends: SAVA vs. Control

Annual deforestation rate (%) across SAVA and non-SAVA communes. Higher values = more forest lost. The vertical dashed line marks the 2015 vanilla price shock. Toggle the vanilla price overlay to see the co-movement.

Total Forest Area Over Time

Vanilla Price Timeline ($/kg)

Approximate export prices showing the massive post-2014 boom.

Treatment Effect: Augmented Synthetic Control

Key result — the causal effect of the 2015 vanilla price shock on deforestation in SAVA communes, estimated via augmented synthetic control. Left panel: actual vs. synthetic SAVA deforestation trajectories. Right panel: the treatment effect (ATT) over time with confidence intervals. Positive values = more deforestation caused by the price shock.

Mean Post-Treatment ATT
—
percentage points
Statistical Significance
—
at 95% level
Pre-Treatment Fit (RMSE)
—
lower = better synthetic match

Scatter: Covariates vs. Deforestation

How do commune characteristics relate to deforestation intensity?

Covariate Matching Explorer — Adjust matching parameters below to see how covariate balance changes. This implements nearest-neighbor matching on the selected covariates. The love plot shows standardized mean differences (SMD) before and after matching. Good balance: |SMD| < 0.1.

Matching Configuration

Covariates:

Love Plot: Standardized Mean Differences

Matched Sample Comparison

Deforestation trends for matched SAVA vs. matched control communes.

Matching Summary

Augmented Synthetic Control Estimates — Positive ATT = more deforestation; Negative ATT = less deforestation. Pre-computed using the augsynth methodology: (1) SCM weights are optimized to minimize pre-treatment RMSE on the aggregate SAVA outcome, (2) Ridge regression augments the counterfactual to debias. Bootstrap SEs are computed by resampling treated units. Donor pool = top 150 control communes by pre-period fit.

Actual vs. Synthetic SAVA

Aggregate SAVA deforestation rate vs. the augmented synthetic control counterfactual. The gap after the treatment year is the estimated causal effect.

ATT (Treatment Effect) Over Time

Gap between actual and synthetic SAVA = estimated ATT. Pre-treatment effects should be near zero (validating the counterfactual). Shaded region = confidence interval.

Robustness: ATT Across Treatment Years

Mean post-treatment ATT assuming different treatment timing. A spike unique to 2015 supports the identification strategy.

Event Study (Period-by-Period Effects)

ATT by year, shown as bars. Pre-treatment bars should be near zero; post-treatment bars show dynamic effects.

Sensitivity: Ridge Regularization (λ)

How does the mean post-treatment ATT change as λ varies? λ=0 is pure SCM, higher λ shrinks the augmentation correction.

Sensitivity: Pre-Period Length

How does the estimate change with different pre-treatment windows? Stable estimates = robust identification.

Top Donor Weights

Control communes receiving the highest SCM weights for the selected specification. These communes form the synthetic SAVA.

Interactive Map of Madagascar Communes

Commune locations based on 350+ geocoded reference points with KNN interpolation for remaining communes. SAVA (treated) communes are in NE Madagascar. Circle size and color reflect deforestation metrics. Click any commune for details.

Covariate Space View

Communes in environmental covariate space. Complements the map above for exploring spatial patterns.

Distribution of Commune-Level Augsynth Treatment Effects

Histogram and kernel density estimate (KDE) of per-commune augmented synthetic control ATTs. Each SAVA commune gets its own synthetic counterfactual built from weighted control communes + ridge augmentation. Positive = vanilla price shock caused more deforestation than the counterfactual. Dashed lines show the mean (purple) and median (green).

Augsynth ATT by Elevation

Mean per-commune augsynth treatment effect grouped by elevation band.

Augsynth ATT by Forest Cover

Mean per-commune augsynth treatment effect grouped by initial forest cover.
